title: Upon Center, A Spectacle-Maker With A Flat Nose.
content:
Center is known weak-sighted, and he sells
    To others store of helpful spectacles.
    Why wears he none? Because we may suppose,
    Where leaven wants, there level lies the nose.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "Upon Center, A Spectacle-Maker With A Flat Nose."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
